questions.title: A conventional morality deals with the level of custom at different levels which is also a branch of______.
questions.explanation:
Conventional morality pertains to the norms, customs, and rules established by society to guide behavior, which falls under the study of ethics.

Ethics is the branch of philosophy that examines moral principles, including societal conventions, to determine what is right or wrong.

questions.explanation:
The level at which an individual judges "what is right and what is wrong" is referred to as the level of morality.

Morality pertains to an individual's principles and values that guide their sense of right and wrong.

questions.title: The moral judgment has tended to deal with the inner causes of action rather than the outward____?
questions.explanation:
Moral judgment often focuses on the inner causes of action (such as intentions, motives, or character) rather than merely judging the outward conduct.

This aligns with ethical theories that emphasize the importance of internal morality over external actions alone.

questions.title: The most national holistically minded today would admit that we have some ____ to all humanity
______has been defined as the normative science of conduct, and conduct is a collective name for voluntary action.

It studies what is right and wrong in human behavior and provides guidelines for making moral decisions.

questions.title: Ethics deals with the standards by which we judge human action to be_________ or wrong.
questions.explanation:
Ethics deals with the standards by which we judge human actions to be right or wrong.

It focuses on determining what is morally acceptable or unacceptable based on principles and values.

questions.title: In 20th century, normative ethics became more complex as they are not only concerned rightness and wrongness but also interested in different kinds of moral

This includes principles like:

  • Justice
  • Fairness
  • Equality
  • Personal virtues

questions.explanation:
The statement emphasizes that ethics critically examines or tests intuitions to check their consistency and compares them with others' intuitions.

This process ensures logical coherence and critical understanding, which is central to ethical inquiry.

questions.explanation:

questions.title: Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) was elected as caliph at which place?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) was elected as the first caliph of Islam at Saqifah Bani Saedah, a place in Madinah, after the death of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in 632 CE.

This event is known as the "Saqifah of Bani Saedah" or "The Gathering of Bani Saedah".

Saqifah Bani Saedah was a covered platform or a roofed building where the Ansar (helpers) gathered to discuss the succession of the Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

The Muhajirun (migrants) later joined them, and after some discussion, Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A) was elected as the caliph.

questions.title: What was the title of Al-Farabi?
questions.explanation:
Al-Farabi was known as the "Second Teacher" or "Magister Secundus".

He was an Arabic philosopher, Islamic scholar, and logician who lived during the Islamic Golden Age.

His work influenced science and philosophy for centuries. 

Some of Al-Farabi's notable works include:

  • Kitab al-Musiqi al-Kabir ("Grand Book of Music")
  • Ara Ahl al-Madina al-Fadila ("Virtuous City")
  • Kitab Ihsa al-Ulum ("Enumeration of the Sciences")
  • Risalah fi'l-Aql (Epistle on the Intellect)

Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

  • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
  • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

  • The five prescribed daily prayers.
  • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).

questions.title: The Miracle of the camel was related to which Prophet?
questions.explanation:
The Miracle of the Camel is a well-known story in Islamic tradition, associated with Prophet Saleh (Hazrat Saleh).

According to the story, Prophet Saleh was sent to the tribe of Thamud, who were known for their wrongdoing.

As a sign of his prophethood, Allah commanded Saleh to ask the tribe to slaughter a pregnant camel, which they refused to do.

In response, Allah saved Saleh and his followers, while the tribe of Thamud was punished.

questions.title: Name the Masjid (Mosque) in which Holy prophet Muhammad (SAW) led the prayer during Miraaj?
questions.explanation:
During the event of Miraaj, the Holy Prophet Muhammad (SAW) led the prayer at Masjid-e-Aqsa, which is located in Jerusalem.


The Miraj event took place on the 27th of Rajab in the 10th (621 A.D) year of Prophethood.

Prophet Muhammad (SAW) was staying at the house of Umm Hani on the night of Miraj.

That night, Angel Jibreel came to the Prophet (SAW) and took him on the journey of Israa and Miraj.

During Miraj, Hazrat Adam and Hazrat Muhammad (SAW) met at the first heaven.

The event is mentioned in Surah Bani Israel.

It is also known as 'Isra and Mi'raj' or 'Shab-e-Meraj'. 

  • Isra: Night journey from Makkah to Masjid Al-Aqsa.
  • Mi’raj: Journey from Masjid Al-Aqsa to the heavens.

Gifts of the 27th of Rajab:

  • The five prescribed daily prayers.
  • The last part of the chapter of Al-Baqara (Amanar-Rasulu).

questions.title: Which Surah of the Holy Quran confirms the Islamic Religion as the complete Religion?
questions.explanation:
Surah Al-Maidah (Chapter 5) of the Holy Quran confirms Islam as the complete and final religion, as stated in Verse 3:

  •  "This day, I have perfected your religion for you, completed My favor upon you, and have chosen for you Islam as your religion." (Quran 5:3)

This verse emphasizes that Islam is the final and complete religion, superseding all previous religions and completing the message of Allah.

questions.title: حضرت امام ابن منبہ نے الصحیفہ الصحیحہ میں کس کی احادیث جمع کی ہیں ؟
questions.answer: حضرت ابو ہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ
questions.explanation: حضرت ابو ہریرہ رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت کردہ احادیث کو الصحیفہ الصحیحہ میں جمع کیا گیا ہے۔

questions.title: حد قذف کا ذکر کس سورہ میں ہے ؟
questions.explanation:
حد قذف کا ذکر ہے: سوره نور


سورہ نور کی آیت 4 میں حد قذف (جھوٹا الزام لگانے) کے بارے میں واضح حکم دیا گیا ہے۔

questions.title: لِلْفُقَرَآءِ الْمُهٰجِرِیْنَ الَّذِیْنَ اُخْرِجُوْا مِنْ دِیَارِهِمْ سے کون سی نص مراد ہے؟
questions.explanation:
لِلْفُقَرَآءِ الْمُهٰجِرِیْنَ الَّذِیْنَ اُخْرِجُوْا مِنْ دِیَارِهِمْ سے مراد ہے:عبارة النص


کیونکہ یہ آیت واضح اور صریح الفاظ میں مہاجرین فقہاء کا ذکر کر رہی ہے جنہیں ان کے گھروں سے نکالا گیا تھا۔ عبارت النص وہ ہوتی ہے جس میں کوئی حکم یا بات براہ راست اور صریح انداز میں بیان کی گئی ہو، جیسے اس آیت میں فقیر مہاجرین کا ذکر کیا گیا ہے۔

questions.title: وَ أُبْرِئُ الْأَكْمَةِ وَ الْأَبْرَصَ میں کس پیغمبر کے معجزات ہیں ؟
questions.explanation: یہ معجزات حضرت عیسیٰ علیہ السلام کے ہیں، جن میں وہ اندھے (اکمہ) اور برص کے مریض (ابرص) کو صحت عطا کرتے تھے

questions.title: زاد المعاد في هدی خیر العباد کا موضوع کیا ہے ؟
questions.explanation:
اد المعاد في هدی خیر العباد کا موضوع  سیرت ہے


یہ کتاب امام ابن قیم الجوزیہ کی لکھی ہوئی ہے اور اس میں نبی اکرم صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم کی سیرت، تعلیمات، اور اخلاقی خصوصیات کا تفصیلی بیان کیا گیا ہے۔

questions.title: دا علمهم بالحلال والحرام کون سے صحابی مراد ہیں؟
questions.explanation: یہ حدیث حضرت معاذ بن جبل رضی اللہ عنہ کے بارے میں ہے، جو علمِ حلال و حرام میں ماہر تھے اور رسول اللہ صلی اللہ علیہ وآلہ وسلم نے انہیں علم سکھایا۔

questions.title: How many times the word ‘Qul’ has been repeated in the Holy Quran?
questions.explanation:
The word "Qul" (قُلْ) is an Arabic word that means "Say" or "Proclaim", and it is a command to the Prophet Muhammad (SAW) to convey the message of Allah.

It has been repeated 332 times in the Holy Quran.


There are 5 Surah in Holy Quran which start with the word Qul:

1.      Al-Jinn(72),

2.      Al-Kafiroon(109),

3.      Al-Ikhlas(112),

4.      Al-Falaq(113),

5.      Al-Naas(114)

questions.title: Who among the following was the first Asian translate the Holy Quran into English?
questions.explanation:
Abdullah Yusuf Ali (1872-1953) was a British-Indian Islamic scholar and translation.

He was best known for his English translation of the Quran, "The Meaning of the Holy Quran".

Published in 1934, it was the first English translation of the Quran by an Asian scholar.

questions.title: نبی کریم ملی ایم نے دس سال عہد میں کتنا علاقہ فتح کیا ؟
questions.explanation:
نبی کریم ﷺ کے دس سالہ دورِ مدنیہ میں تقریباً سوا لاکھ مربع میل علاقہ اسلام کے زیرِ نگین آیا تھا۔

اس عرصے میں اسلامی ریاست کا دائرہ مکہ، مدینہ، خیبر، طائف، اور دیگر قریبی علاقوں تک پھیل چکا تھا۔

اس کے ساتھ ہی ارد گرد کے علاقوں میں بھی اسلام کا پیغام پہنچا اور کئی قبائل نے اسلام قبول کیا۔

یہ فتوحات اور پھیلاؤ اس حکمتِ عملی اور عادلانہ طرزِ حکمرانی کی بنیاد پر ہوا جو نبی کریم ﷺ نے اپنایا تھا،

جس میں دینِ اسلام کا فروغ اور اخلاقی تعلیمات کا نفاذ شامل تھا۔

questions.title: احکام عشرہ کا ذکر کون سی سورۃ میں موجود ہے؟
questions.explanation:
سورة الانعام - آیت 152

موسیٰ علیہ السلام کو کوہ طور پر لکھی لکھائی تختیاں دی گئیں۔
ان میں دس احکام مذکور تھے جنہیں احکام عشرہ کہتے ہیں
۔ یہ احکام بعد میں تورات میں شامل کردیئے گئے۔
ان دس احکام میں سے ایک حکم سبت کے دن کی تعظیم تھا۔
اگر اسے نکال دیا جائے تو باقی یہی نو احکام رہ جاتے ہیں جو ان آیات میں مذکور ہیں

questions.title: کسی مسجد کی بنیاد تقوی پر رکھی گئی ہے ؟
questions.explanation:
مسجد قباء مسلمانوں کے دلوں میں بڑا مقام رکھتی ہے:

ارشاد باری تعالیٰ ہے: ''جس مسجد کی بنیاد پہلے دن سے تقویٰ پر رکھی گئی ہ ہو زیادہ حقدار ہے کہ آپ اس میں کھڑے ہوں اس میں وہ لوگ ہیں جو پاکیزگی کو پسند کرتے ہیں، اور اللہ تعالیٰ پاکیزگی والوں کو پسند کرتا ہے۔''

Hazrat Usman (RA) at the persuasion of Hazrat Abu-Bakr (RA) accepted Islam.

Wives of Hazrat Usman (RA) namely Ruqayyah (RA) & Umm-e-Kulthum (RA) were the daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH).

Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on deathbed.

After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

Ruqayyah (RA died on victory day of Battle of Badr.

Hazrat Usman (RA) purchased the adjoining land for 25,000 dirham to extend Masjid-e-Nabavi.

Hazrat Usman used to enfranchise a slave on every Friday.

Muslim Navy was also developed during the period of Hazrat Usman (RA).

 Zun-Noorain means Possessor of Two Lights

Jame-al-Quran means compiler of Quran.

questions.title: A person who is liable to pay Zakat is called __________?
questions.explanation:
The nisab by the gold standard is 3 ounces of gold (87.48 grammes) or its equivalent in cash.

The nisab by the silver standard is 21 ounces of silver (612.36 grammes) or its equivalent in cash.

questions.title: Who was the sixth caliph of the Umayyad dynasty during the Debal incident?
questions.explanation:
Walid bin Abdul Malik was the sixth caliph of the Umayyad dynasty, ruling from 705 to 715 CE.

During his reign, the Debal incident occurred in 711 CE, when the Arab governor of Sindh, Muhammad bin Qasim, conquered the port city of Debal (now Karachi) in modern-day Pakistan.

questions.title: Who interpreted neo-Sufi concept of Islam?
questions.explanation:
Shah Wali-Ullah (1703-1762) was a prominent Islamic scholar.

He was a reformer who interpreted the neo-Sufi concept of Islam.

He was a member of the Naqshbandi Sufi order.

Shah Wali-Ullah emphasized the importance of inner spiritual growth, purification of the heart, and the pursuit of knowledge.

questions.title: The Paradise has ________ doors.
questions.explanation:
Paradise (Jannah) is described as having eight doors in Islamic teachings.

Each door represents different acts of worship, such as prayer (Salat), charity (Zakat), fasting (Sawm), Hajj, jihad, and others.

questions.title: The name of the mosque established by “Munafiqeen” in Madina was _____?
questions.explanation:
The mosque established by the "Munafiqeen" (hypocrites) in Madina was called Masjid al-Zarar.

It was built with malicious intentions, and Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) ordered its destruction in 9 Hijri after its true purpose was revealed.

questions.title: The Prophet (SW) remained in Mecca after Nubuwat for _______ years.
questions.explanation:
The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) remained in Mecca for 13 years after receiving prophethood (Nubuwat).

During this period, he preached Islam before migrating to Medina in 622 CE (Hijrah).

questions.title: Whose son was Hazrat Ikram R.A?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Ikrimah R.A was the son of Abu Jehal.

He was a prominent leader of the Quraysh tribe who opposed Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

After initially opposing Islam, Ikrimah later embraced the faith and became a companion of the Prophet (PBUH).

questions.title: In which battle did 70 Muslims embrace martyrdom?
questions.explanation:
• In the Battle of Uhud, 70 Muslims were martyred.
  • The Battle of Uhud occurred on Saturday, 23 March 625 AD (3 Hijri) in the valley north of Mount Uhud.
    • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) led around 1000 Muslims in the Battle of Uhud.
    • They faced 3000 Qurayshi Meccans commanded by Abu Sufyan ibn Harb.
    • Abdullah bin Jubair was appointed by Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) to lead the 50 skilled archers positioned on Uhud Mountain.

questions.title: Which surah of the Holy Quran is named after Honey Bee?
questions.explanation:
There are more than 200 verses in the Quran dealing with animals

The biggest animal mentioned in Holy Quran Elephant.
The smallest animal mentioned in Holy Quran Mosquito.

The six chapters of the Quran are named after animals or insects:

1.      Surat 2, Al Baqarah (The Cow);

2.      Surat 6, Al Anaam (The Cattle),

3.      Surat 16, Al Nahl (The Bees);

4.      Surat 27, Al Naml (The Ants);

5.      Surat 29, Al Ankabut (The Spider);

6.      Surat 105, Al Fil.

questions.explanation:
•  Hazrat Yousuf (AS) had knowledge of the interpretation of dreams.

questions.title: Which Umayyad caliph initiated the conquest of Spain?
questions.explanation:
In 711 Muslim forces invaded and in seven years conquered the Iberian peninsula.

It became one of the great Muslim civilizations; reaching its summit with the Umayyad caliphate of Cordovain the tenth century. Muslim rule declined after that and ended in 1492 when Granada was conquered.

questions.title: What was the 1st civil war in Islam?
questions.explanation:
The Battle of the Camel, also known as the Battle of Jamel or the Battle of Basra,

Battle of Jamel took place outside of Basra, Iraq, in 36 AH/656 CE.

The battle was fought between the army of the fourth caliph, Hazrat Ali رضہ, on one side, and the other army led by Hazrat Aisha رضہ,

questions.title: Usher and Khums are _____?
questions.answer: Two kinds of Tax in Islam
questions.explanation:
Usher and Khums are Two kinds of Tax in Islam,

In Islam, KHUMS خمس refers to the required religious obligation of any Muslims to pay one-fifth of their acquired wealth from certain sources toward specified causes. Khums is different and separate from other Islamic taxes such as zakat and jizya.

questions.title: Backbiting is like eating flesh of one's dead _______?
questions.explanation:
Hazrat Abu Hurayrah, has quoted Prophet Muhammadﷺ, as saying:

"Do you know what backbiting is?" They said, "Allah and His Messenger know best." He then said, "It is to say something about your brother that he would dislike." Someone asked him, "But what if what I say is true?" Allah’s Messenger said, "If what you say about him is true, you are backbiting him, but if it is not true then you have slandered him."

questions.title: In which Surah of the Holy Quran, the word "Khatimun Nabiyen" is mentioned?
questions.explanation:
Al-Ahzab is the 33rd chapter of the Quran with 73 verses.

It is named after the confederates who fought the Muslims at the Battle of the Trench.

It is also known as the Battle of the Parties or the Siege of Madina.
